 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1ohe","title":"[Epic] E2E Verbose Logging Enhancement","description":"## Epic: E2E Verbose Logging Enhancement

Enhance E2E test logging beyond basic JSONL events to include detailed debugging information, performance metrics, and failure context.

### Goals
1. **Every test failure is debuggable from logs alone** - No need to re-run locally
2. **Performance regressions are detectable** - Timing data for critical operations
3. **Resource leaks are traceable** - Memory, file handles, temp files

### Current State
- Basic JSONL events: run_start, test_start, test_end, run_end
- Missing: verbose step-by-step output, metrics, failure state dumps

### Features in This Epic

1. **Verbose Debug Logging Mode** (395v)
   - `E2E_VERBOSE=1` environment variable
   - Detailed step-by-step logs to separate file
   - Does not affect JSONL event stream

2. **Performance Metrics Collection** (1bqi)
   - Emit `metrics` events with timing data
   - Track: indexer duration, search latency, memory usage, index size

3. **Failure State Dump** (rtpd)
   - On panic/failure, automatically dump:
     - Environment variables
     - Temp directory listing
     - Last 100 lines of logs
     - Database state

### Success Metrics
- 100% of test failures can be triaged from CI logs
- P95 operation times available in test-results/
- Resource leak reports generated automatically","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:23:59.502864770Z","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:23:34.832703992Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T23:23:34.832529397Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1tmi","title":"[Task] Opt 7.4: Benchmark SQLite ID caching","description":"## Objective
Benchmark the performance improvement from SQLite ID caching.

## Benchmark Scenarios

### 1. Indexing Throughput
```rust
#[bench]
fn bench_indexing_with_cache(b: &mut Bencher) {
    let corpus = generate_corpus(3000);
    b.iter(|| {
        // Cache enabled (default)
        index_corpus(&corpus)
    });
}

#[bench]
fn bench_indexing_without_cache(b: &mut Bencher) {
    std::env::set_var("CASS_SQLITE_CACHE", "0");
    let corpus = generate_corpus(3000);
    b.iter(|| {
        index_corpus(&corpus)
    });
    std::env::remove_var("CASS_SQLITE_CACHE");
}
```

### 2. Syscall Profiling
```bash
# With cache disabled
CASS_SQLITE_CACHE=0 strace -c cargo test -- test_index_corpus

# With cache enabled
strace -c cargo test -- test_index_corpus
```

Compare:
- pwrite64 count
- pread64 count
- Total syscall count

### 3. Cache Hit Ratio
Log cache hits/misses during indexing to verify effectiveness:
- Expected: >90% hit ratio for agent_ids
- Expected: >80% hit ratio for workspace_ids

## Success Criteria
- Indexing time reduction (measure actual %)
- SQLite syscalls reduced by 30-50%
- No memory overhead concerns (cache is small)

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2w4","title":"P1.1 Define Source/Origin Rust types","description":"# Define Source/Origin Rust Types

## Context
We need Rust types to represent "where a conversation came from" - the provenance system. These types will be used throughout cass: storage, indexing, search, CLI, TUI.

## Location
Create new module: src/model/source.rs (or src/sources.rs if we prefer top-level)

## Types to Define

### SourceKind enum
\`\`\`rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum SourceKind {
    Local,
    Ssh,
    // Future: S3, Git, Http
}
\`\`\`

### Source struct (configuration-level)
\`\`\`rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Source {
    /// Stable, user-friendly identifier (e.g., "local", "work-laptop", "home-server")
    pub id: String,
    
    /// What type of source this is
    pub kind: SourceKind,
    
    /// Display label for UI (often SSH alias or hostname)
    pub host_label: Option<String>,
    
    /// Optional stable machine identifier (hashed if desired)
    pub machine_id: Option<String>,
    
    /// Platform hint for path defaults
    pub platform: Option<Platform>,
    
    /// Arbitrary config (SSH params, path rewrites, etc)
    pub config: serde_json::Value,
    
    pub created_at: Option<i64>,
    pub updated_at: Option<i64>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum Platform {
    Macos,
    Linux,
    Windows,
}
\`\`\`

### Origin struct (per-conversation metadata)
\`\`\`rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Origin {
    /// References Source.id
    pub source_id: String,
    
    /// Denormalized for convenience
    pub kind: SourceKind,
    
    /// Display host label (may differ from source's host_label)
    pub host: Option<String>,
}

impl Default for Origin {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            source_id: "local".into(),
            kind: SourceKind::Local,
            host: None,
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Constants
\`\`\`rust
pub const LOCAL_SOURCE_ID: &str = "local";
\`\`\`

## Considerations
- Use serde for JSON serialization (robot output compatibility)
- Derive common traits: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash where appropriate
- Document public types with doc comments
- Consider implementing Display for user-friendly formatting

## Wire into lib.rs
Export these types from the crate root so they're accessible:
\`\`\`rust
pub mod sources;
pub use sources::{Source, SourceKind, Origin, Platform, LOCAL_SOURCE_ID};
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Types defined with proper derives
- [ ] Doc comments on all public items
- [ ] Unit tests for Default impls and serialization
- [ ] Exported from crate root
- [ ] cargo check passes
- [ ] cargo clippy passes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-16T05:54:10.114500Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T06:51:13.559723Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T06:51:13.559723Z","close_reason":"Implemented SourceKind, Source, Origin, SourceFilter types with LOCAL_SOURCE_ID constant in src/sources/provenance.rs. ## Objective

Before implementing streaming canonicalization, understand the current implementation and all allocation sites.

## From PLAN Section 4.3: Canonicalization Hotspot

Location: `canonicalize.rs:80-95`

```rust
pub fn canonicalize_for_embedding(text: &str) -> String {
    let normalized: String = text.nfc().collect();  // Allocation #1
    let stripped = strip_markdown_and_code(&normalized);  // Allocation #2
    let whitespace_normalized = normalize_whitespace(&stripped);  // Allocation #3
    let filtered = filter_low_signal(&whitespace_normalized);  // Allocation #4
    truncate_to_chars(&filtered, MAX_EMBED_CHARS)  // Allocation #5
}
```

   - `strip_markdown_and_code()` - Remove markdown/code blocks
   - `normalize_whitespace()` - Collapse whitespace runs

   - `truncate_to_chars()` - Limit to MAX_EMBED_CHARS

## Objective

## Benchmark Protocol

### 1. Micro-Benchmark: Regex Build Time

```rust
// In benches/runtime_perf.rs
fn bench_regex_build(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let pattern = "*substring*";
    
    c.bench_function("regex_build_uncached", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            REGEX_CACHE.lock().unwrap().clear();
            build_regex_query("content", black_box(pattern))
        })
    });
    
    c.bench_function("regex_build_cached", |b| {
        // Pre-populate cache
        build_regex_query("content", pattern);
        
        b.iter(|| {
            build_regex_query("content", black_box(pattern))
        })
    });
}
```

### 2. End-to-End Wildcard Search Benchmark
```bash
# Baseline (cache disabled)
export CASS_REGEX_CACHE=0
cargo bench --bench runtime_perf -- wildcard --save-baseline cache_disabled

# With cache

# Compare
critcmp cache_disabled cache_enabled
```

### 3. Repeated Query Benchmark
```rust
fn bench_repeated_wildcard(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let index = create_large_test_index();
    let patterns = ["*error*", "*TODO*", "*FIXME*", "*warning*"];
    
    c.bench_function("repeated_wildcards_10x", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            for _ in 0..10 {
                for pattern in &patterns {
                    let _ = search_wildcard(&index, pattern);
                }
            }
        })
    });
}
```

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-uile","title":"Opt 3.1: Binary Metadata Serialization (50-70% storage reduction)","description":"# Optimization 3.1: Binary Metadata Serialization (50-70% storage reduction)

## Summary
Metadata is stored as JSON text in SQLite, which is verbose and slow to parse.
Binary formats like MessagePack offer 50-70% storage reduction and faster
serialization while maintaining schema evolution support.

## Location
- **File:** src/storage/sqlite.rs
- **Lines:** Schema definition, metadata storage, ConversationMetadata struct
- **Related:** Message storage, search result hydration

## Current State
\`\`\`rust
// Stored as JSON text in SQLite TEXT column
let json = serde_json::to_string(&metadata)?;
conn.execute("INSERT INTO messages (metadata, ...) VALUES (?)", [json])?;

// Reading
let json: String = row.get("metadata")?;
let metadata: ConversationMetadata = serde_json::from_str(&json)?;
\`\`\`

## Problem Analysis
1. **Verbose storage:** JSON field names repeated per row (30-50% overhead)
2. **Slow parsing:** Text parsing slower than binary decode
3. **Size overhead:** Numbers as text, Unicode escaping, whitespace
4. **Index bloat:** Larger column values increase B-tree size

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Binary Format Selection: MessagePack (Recommended)
\`\`\`rust
// Cargo.toml
// rmp-serde = "1"

use rmp_serde as rmps;

// Schema change: metadata column to BLOB, add version byte
#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
struct VersionedMetadata {
    #[serde(rename = "v")]
    version: u8,
    #[serde(flatten)]
    data: ConversationMetadata,
}

const METADATA_FORMAT_VERSION: u8 = 1;

fn serialize_metadata(metadata: &ConversationMetadata) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
    let versioned = VersionedMetadata {
        version: METADATA_FORMAT_VERSION,
        data: metadata.clone(),
    };
    rmps::to_vec(&versioned).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("msgpack encode: {}", e))
}

fn deserialize_metadata(bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<ConversationMetadata> {
    if bytes.is_empty() {
        return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("empty metadata"));
    }
    
    // Version check for future format migrations
    let versioned: VersionedMetadata = rmps::from_slice(bytes)
        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("msgpack decode: {}", e))?;
    
    if versioned.version != METADATA_FORMAT_VERSION {
        // Handle future version migrations here
        tracing::warn!(
            found = versioned.version,
            expected = METADATA_FORMAT_VERSION,
            "Metadata version mismatch"
        );
    }
    
    Ok(versioned.data)
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Dual-Read During Migration
\`\`\`rust
fn read_metadata_compat(row: &Row) -> Result<ConversationMetadata> {
    // Try binary column first (new format)
    if let Ok(bytes) = row.get::<_, Vec<u8>>("metadata_bin") {
        return deserialize_metadata(&bytes);
    }
    
    // Fall back to JSON column (old format)
    if let Ok(json) = row.get::<_, String>("metadata") {
        return serde_json::from_str(&json)
            .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("json decode: {}", e));
    }
    
    Err(anyhow::anyhow!("no metadata found"))
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Schema Migration Strategy
\`\`\`sql
-- Migration v6 -> v7: Add binary metadata column
-- Phase 1: Add new column alongside old
ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN metadata_bin BLOB;

-- Phase 2: Batch migration (in Rust)
-- SELECT rowid, metadata FROM messages WHERE metadata_bin IS NULL LIMIT 1000;
-- For each row: convert JSON -> binary, UPDATE ... SET metadata_bin = ?

-- Phase 3: After all data migrated, make binary primary
-- (Optional) DROP COLUMN metadata; -- if supported

-- Index on new column if needed for queries
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_has_binary 
    ON messages(rowid) WHERE metadata_bin IS NOT NULL;
\`\`\`

\`\`\`rust
/// Batch migration function
pub fn migrate_metadata_to_binary(conn: &Connection) -> Result<usize> {
    let batch_size = 1000;
    let mut total_migrated = 0;
    
    loop {
        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(
            "SELECT rowid, metadata FROM messages 
             WHERE metadata IS NOT NULL AND metadata_bin IS NULL 
             LIMIT ?"
        )?;
        
        let rows: Vec<(i64, String)> = stmt
            .query_map([batch_size], |row| {
                Ok((row.get(0)?, row.get(1)?))
            })?
            .filter_map(|r| r.ok())
            .collect();
        
        if rows.is_empty() {
            break;
        }
        
        let tx = conn.transaction()?;
        for (rowid, json) in &rows {
            let metadata: ConversationMetadata = serde_json::from_str(json)?;
            let binary = serialize_metadata(&metadata)?;
            tx.execute(
                "UPDATE messages SET metadata_bin = ? WHERE rowid = ?",
                rusqlite::params![binary, rowid],
            )?;
        }
        tx.commit()?;
        
        total_migrated += rows.len();
        tracing::info!(migrated = rows.len(), total = total_migrated, "Migrating metadata");
    }
    
    Ok(total_migrated)
}
\`\`\`

## Implementation Steps
1. [ ] Add rmp-serde to Cargo.toml
2. [ ] Create VersionedMetadata wrapper struct
3. [ ] Implement serialize/deserialize functions
4. [ ] Add migration v6 -> v7 with new column
5. [ ] Implement batch migration function
6. [ ] Update write path to use binary format
7. [ ] Update read path with dual-column support
8. [ ] Benchmark storage size and parse speed
9. [ ] Add JSON export for debugging/troubleshooting

## Comprehensive Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    
    fn sample_metadata() -> ConversationMetadata {
        ConversationMetadata {
            agent: "claude".to_string(),
            workspace: "/home/user/project".to_string(),
            created_at: 1704067200000,
            session_id: Some("abc123".to_string()),
            tags: vec!["rust".to_string(), "optimization".to_string()],
            ..Default::default()
        }
    }
    
    /// Roundtrip serialization preserves data
    #[test]
    fn test_roundtrip() {
        let original = sample_metadata();
        let bytes = serialize_metadata(&original).unwrap();
        let recovered = deserialize_metadata(&bytes).unwrap();
        
        assert_eq!(original.agent, recovered.agent);
        assert_eq!(original.workspace, recovered.workspace);
        assert_eq!(original.created_at, recovered.created_at);
        assert_eq!(original.session_id, recovered.session_id);
        assert_eq!(original.tags, recovered.tags);
    }
    
    /// Binary format is smaller than JSON
    #[test]
    fn test_size_reduction() {
        let metadata = sample_metadata();
        
        let json = serde_json::to_string(&metadata).unwrap();
        let binary = serialize_metadata(&metadata).unwrap();
        
        let reduction = (json.len() as f64 - binary.len() as f64) / json.len() as f64 * 100.0;
        
        println!("JSON size: {} bytes", json.len());
        println!("Binary size: {} bytes", binary.len());
        println!("Reduction: {:.1}%", reduction);
        
        assert!(binary.len() < json.len(), "Binary should be smaller");
        assert!(reduction >= 30.0, "Should achieve at least 30% reduction");
    }
    
    /// Empty metadata roundtrips correctly
    #[test]
    fn test_empty_metadata() {
        let original = ConversationMetadata::default();
        let bytes = serialize_metadata(&original).unwrap();
        let recovered = deserialize_metadata(&bytes).unwrap();
        
        assert_eq!(original, recovered);
    }
    
    /// Large metadata (many tags)
    #[test]
    fn test_large_metadata() {
        let mut metadata = sample_metadata();
        metadata.tags = (0..100).map(|i| format!("tag_{}", i)).collect();
        
        let bytes = serialize_metadata(&metadata).unwrap();
        let recovered = deserialize_metadata(&bytes).unwrap();
        
        assert_eq!(metadata.tags.len(), recovered.tags.len());
    }
    
    /// Unicode content preserved
    #[test]
    fn test_unicode_content() {
        let mut metadata = sample_metadata();
        metadata.workspace = "/home/用户/项目".to_string();
        metadata.tags = vec!["日本語".to_string(), "emoji🔥".to_string()];
        
        let bytes = serialize_metadata(&metadata).unwrap();
        let recovered = deserialize_metadata(&bytes).unwrap();
        
        assert_eq!(metadata.workspace, recovered.workspace);
        assert_eq!(metadata.tags, recovered.tags);
    }
    
    /// Invalid binary returns error
    #[test]
    fn test_invalid_binary() {
        let garbage = vec![0xFF, 0xFE, 0x00, 0x01];
        let result = deserialize_metadata(&garbage);
        assert!(result.is_err());
    }
    
    /// Empty input returns error
    #[test]
    fn test_empty_input() {
        let result = deserialize_metadata(&[]);
        assert!(result.is_err());
    }

fn test_dual_read_compatibility() {

    // Insert binary-only row
    conn.execute(
        "INSERT INTO messages (metadata_bin) VALUES (?)",
        [&binary],
    ).unwrap();
    
    // Insert both columns
    conn.execute(
        "INSERT INTO messages (metadata, metadata_bin) VALUES (?, ?)",
        rusqlite::params![&json, &binary],
    ).unwrap();
    
    // Read all three and verify
    let mut stmt = conn.prepare("SELECT * FROM messages").unwrap();
    let rows: Vec<_> = stmt.query_map([], |row| {
        Ok(read_metadata_compat(row).unwrap())
    }).unwrap().collect();
    
    assert_eq!(rows.len(), 3);
    for recovered in rows {
        let recovered = recovered.unwrap();
        assert_eq!(recovered.agent, metadata.agent);

    }

    assert_eq!(migrated, 100);

    let count: i64 = conn.query_row(
        "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM messages WHERE metadata_bin IS NOT NULL",
        [],
        |r| r.get(0),
    ).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(count, 100);
    
    // Verify data integrity
    let mut stmt = conn.prepare("SELECT metadata_bin FROM messages").unwrap();
    for row in stmt.query_map([], |r| r.get::<_, Vec<u8>>(0)).unwrap() {
        let bytes = row.unwrap();
        let metadata = deserialize_metadata(&bytes).unwrap();
        assert!(!metadata.agent.is_empty());
    }

/// Full E2E with real database
#[test]
#[ignore] // Run with --include-ignored
fn test_e2e_binary_metadata() {
    use tempfile::TempDir;
    
    let temp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
    let db_path = temp.path().join("test.db");
    
    // Initialize database with schema v7
    let mut storage = SqliteStorage::open(&db_path).unwrap();
    
    // Insert messages with binary metadata
    for i in 0..100 {
        let mut metadata = sample_metadata();
        metadata.session_id = Some(format!("session_{}", i));
        storage.insert_message("test content", &metadata).unwrap();
    }
    
    // Close and reopen
    drop(storage);
    let storage = SqliteStorage::open(&db_path).unwrap();
    
    // Query and verify
    let results = storage.query_all().unwrap();
    assert_eq!(results.len(), 100);
    
    for (i, msg) in results.iter().enumerate() {
        assert_eq!(
            msg.metadata.session_id,
            Some(format!("session_{}", i))
        );
    }
    
    // Verify storage size is reduced
    let file_size = std::fs::metadata(&db_path).unwrap().len();
    println!("Database size: {} KB", file_size / 1024);
}

fn serialize_metadata(metadata: &ConversationMetadata) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
    let start = std::time::Instant::now();
    let versioned = VersionedMetadata {
        version: METADATA_FORMAT_VERSION,
        data: metadata.clone(),
    };
    let bytes = rmps::to_vec(&versioned)?;
    
    tracing::trace!(
        size = bytes.len(),
        elapsed_us = start.elapsed().as_micros(),
        "Serialized metadata to binary"
    );
    
    Ok(bytes)
}

fn migrate_metadata_to_binary(conn: &Connection) -> Result<usize> {
    let start = std::time::Instant::now();

}

## Goal

Build the browser-based viewer that authenticates users, decrypts the payload, loads the SQLite database, and provides search + conversation browsing functionality.

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    Browser Runtime                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ┌───────────────┐ ┌───────────────┐ ┌─────────────────────┐ │
│ │ AuthModule    │ │ CryptoModule  │ │ DatabaseModule      │ │
│ │               │ │               │ │                     │ │
│ │ - Password UI │ │ - Argon2 WASM │ │ - sqlite-wasm       │ │
│ │ - QR scanner  │ │ - AES-GCM     │ │ - FTS5 queries      │ │
│ │ - Session mgmt│ │ - Key storage │ │ - Result rendering  │ │
│ └───────────────┘ └───────────────┘ └─────────────────────┘ │
│ ┌───────────────┐ ┌───────────────┐ ┌─────────────────────┐ │
│ │ SearchUI      │ │ ConversationUI│ │ ExportUI            │ │
│ │               │ │               │ │                     │ │
│ │ - Query input │ │ - Message list│ │ - Copy/download     │ │
│ │ - Filters     │ │ - Syntax hl   │ │ - Share links       │ │
│ │ - Results     │ │ - Navigation  │ │ - Print view        │ │
│ └───────────────┘ └───────────────┘ └─────────────────────┘ │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Worker Architecture

All expensive operations run in a dedicated Web Worker:

```
main thread:                    crypto_worker.js:
  - Auth UI                       - Argon2id derivation
  - Progress display              - DEK unwrapping
  - Rendering                     - Chunk decrypt
                                  - Streaming decompress
                                  - OPFS write
                                  - sqlite-wasm init
```

## Key Technologies

| Library | Purpose | Size (gzip) |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| sqlite-wasm | SQLite in browser (OPFS) | 340KB |
| argon2-browser | Password hashing | 78KB |
| fflate | Streaming decompression | 9KB |
| Marked.js | Markdown rendering | 18KB |
| Prism.js | Syntax highlighting | 11KB |
| DOMPurify | XSS sanitization | 8KB |
| html5-qrcode | QR code scanning | 52KB |

## CSP-Safe UI

No Alpine.js or eval-dependent frameworks. Custom UI layer with:
- No inline event handlers
- No eval() or new Function()
- External CSS only (no inline styles)
- ES modules with proper imports

## File Structure (Assets)

```
src/pages_assets/
├── index.html          # Entry point with CSP meta tag
├── auth.js             # Authentication UI
├── crypto_worker.js    # Decryption worker
├── viewer.js           # Main application
├── search.js           # Search UI component
├── conversation.js     # Conversation renderer
├── styles.css          # Tailwind-based styles
└── vendor/
    ├── sqlite3.js      # sqlite-wasm loader
    ├── sqlite3.wasm
    ├── argon2-wasm.js
    ├── argon2-wasm.wasm
    ├── fflate.min.js
    └── ...
```
